name: Ruyuan
title: Ruyuan Wan
subtitle: Ph.D. student at The Pennsylvania State University

bio.name: Ruyuan Wan
bio.about: I am a second-year Ph.D. student in Information Science and Technology at Pennsylvania State University, working with Dr. Ting-hao 'Kenneth' Huang. Prior to starting my Ph.D., I earned Master’s degrees in Data Science and Linguistics, as well as a Bachelor's degree in Statistics from the University of Minnesota. During that time, I was advised by Dr. Lana Yarosh and Dr. Dongyeop Kang. I also earned a Master degree in Computer Science at the Univeristy of Notre Dame where I studied in the ND-HCI lab.
bio.bio: My research lies at the intersection of Human Computer Interaction (HCI), Natural Language Processing (NLP), and Social Computing. I study how human communication and AI systems co-evolve under algorithmic and interface constraints in complex social contexts. I develop taxonomies, multilingual datasets, and evaluation frameworks–combining LLM benchmarking with qualitative methods–to improve the robustness, fairness, and safety of real-world human-AI systems.

news.news: I am co-organizing the 5th NLP for Positive Impact workshop at EMNLP 2026.
My IBM internship paper got the Best Paper Honorable Mention Award at CHI 2026.
Gave a talk at the University of Chicago on Creative Communication under Constraints.
Gave an invited talk at Microsoft on User Agency and Creative Adaptation on Algorithm-Driven Platforms.
I co-organzied the 4th Workshop on NLP for Positive Impact at ACL 2025.
My first first-authored CHI paper got accepted!
I'm serving as AC for CHI 2025 LBW track.
I co-organzied the Pluralistic Alignment workshop at NeurIPS 2024.
Presented my first oral talk at EMNLP!
